A big restore wave: bring back a whole Docker Compose stack in one go, leave containers stopped after a restore, and multi-select file restore.

## Added
- **Restore a whole Docker Compose stack at once.** Containers from the same compose project (the `com.docker.compose.project` label, which BombVault already captures) are grouped into a new **Stacks** panel above the container list. **Restore stack** rebuilds every member from its latest backup **left stopped**, then optionally starts them in `depends_on` order, so a stack (for example one managed with Dockhand) comes back without members racing ahead of the containers they depend on. It respects each container's run-state, holds back a container whose dependency failed to restore instead of starting it into a broken state, and restores from the stored definitions, so it also works when the whole stack was deleted (disaster recovery). Requested on the forum.
- **"Leave stopped after restore" option.** A checkbox on the container and VM restore recreates/restores without starting it, so you can rebuild a group of dependent containers one by one and start them yourself in the right order.
- **Multi-select file restore with a destination choice.** In a snapshot's **Files** view you can now tick any number of files and folders and restore the whole selection at once, either **in place** (original locations) or **into a folder** you pick. (#21, thanks @manilx)

## Fixed
- **Restore keeps the run-state, consistently.** A container that was running when backed up comes back running; one that was stopped stays stopped. VMs used to always boot on restore regardless of their state at backup; they now mirror the captured run-state too (backups taken before this release still boot, as before).

---

Image: `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/bombvault:3.3.0` (also `:3`, `:3.3`, `:latest`) and `docker.io/junkerderprovinz/bombvault:3.3.0`, multi-arch (amd64 + arm64).
